findings.evidence: `rg` hit: `CircleFundamentalGroup.lean:104:      simpa [T] using h'`; `T` is the composed opposite equivalence defined at lines 96-98 and the result feeds the regular-cover iff at lines 105-107.
findings.fix: Factor the wrapper calculation into a small private lemma characterizing `T.symm` on `n`, or rewrite this step explicitly with `MulEquiv.symm_apply_eq`/the relevant opposite-equivalence simp lemmas so the proof states why the opposite drops out.
findings.issue: `fundamentalGroupMulEquivZMultiples_apply_eq_iff` proves `F γ = MulOpposite.op (Deck.addCircleMulEquiv n)` with `simpa [T] using h'`, relying on simplification through the composed `MulEquiv.op`/`opMulEquiv.symm` wrappers rather than a named fact.

summary: The main characterization proof still hides the opposite-equivalence bookkeeping behind an unfolding-heavy `simpa`, making the proof brittle under changes to equivalence simp normal forms.
